description: The Gangster Disciples are an African-American street and prison gang founded in Chicago in 1966 by Larry Hoover and David Barksdale. Originally formed as the Black Gangster Disciple Nation, the group split in 1989 into the Gangster Disciple Nation and the Black Disciple Nation. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, assault, firearms violations, fraud, homicide, money laundering, and organized crime, with an estimated 30,000 members in Chicago and operations across the United States and internationally.

country_links.evidence_quote: On July 3, 2005, men claiming to be members of the Gangster Disciples street gang killed Sergeant Juwan Johnson of the U.S. Army in the small town of Hohenecken (part of the city of Kaiserslautern) near Ramstein, Germany.
country_links.context: The Gangster Disciples carried out a fatal beating of a U.S. Army sergeant in Hohenecken, Germany, as part of a gang initiation.
